Transcrição Completa: 23 How To PR For Expansion

 

[00:00][music]

 

[00:02]You want to use PR to expand the category and you want to use advertising to retain the attention that was gained from expanding the category. What the hell am I talking about? Let me explain.

 

[00:15]PR press releases, you putting out result driven headlines. When you choose to advertise those towards people that you eventually want to sell and turn into clients, that brings awareness to the category that you are operating in.

 

[00:29]So let's say you're building chat bots. Let's say you're doing Facebook ads or LinkedIn advertising, whatever it is that you're specializing in, right? That being advertised through a press release, you would talk about what you are doing in the space, not in a selfish way, but about the space itself.

 

[00:47]Meaning, if I'm marketing something that I did to get a result for chat bots. Let's say I generated 9x the leads that the person got the month prior and I generated 20x the amount of money they made the month prior just in transitioning from basic lead generation over to chatbot lead generation.

 

[01:07]Well, if I put that in a headline, I might say fitness gyms are gaining 9x leads using chat bots. I wouldn't say Megalodon Marketing got 9x the chatbot leads for this specific business because people won't give a fuck about such a specific result that doesn't talk about the category.

 

[01:28]See, let me explain. If I'm in the fitness niche, if I'm in that industry, if I have a gym, especially being the person that you're wanting to sell in that kind of headline example, I am going to care more about the thing that doesn't have to do with you.

 

[01:43]I'm going to care more about the thing that has to do with the category, the industry that I'm participating in. So therefore, if I'm a gym owner and I read somebody got 9x the leads using a chatbot, let me click this.

 

[01:57]And in that article, over 95% of what what's talked about is the process, how chat bots work, what the hell a chatbot is, and how they can integrated into their business being a gym owner, and how the other gym owner got the result doing it.

 

[02:15]You being mentioned in 5% of it, meaning 95% about the process, about what the hell a chatbot is, about how the gym owner got the result. It might mention one sentence out of the entire press release that says, "And that result was gotten by this specific agency or that result was gotten by this specific digital marketer."

 

[02:36]That's all that needs to be said. That one sentence can have a hyperlink over to your name that that can have a hyperlink over to your agency's website. That's all that needs to happen in a press release.

 

[02:48]The press release needs to focus on expanding the category, talking about the industry, getting more people in the industry interested in what is being provided, not by the person who provided it. The person who provided it and did it and got the result is mentioned and hyperlinked to. That's how press works.

 

[03:09]Okay, that press article, you're probably like, well, why would I do that? You know, instead of running a lead form on Facebook and and generating clients for my business that way, that's not the way to do it because you won't gain expert positioning at the same time.

 

[03:25]You got to make somebody aware of a result that they could get and be mentioned because it doesn't it it's not interpreted as an advertisement to the person who's reading it as the gym owner.

 

[03:37]See, if the gym owner sees an ad on Facebook as they're scrolling through their feed that says get more results with chat bots will help. Although you might generate some leads with that, they're not going to be as qualified and you're not going to get as many gym owner eyeballs on the ad as you would with a press release because once again, a press release is interpreted differently in the mind.

 

[04:00]A press release isn't usually interpreted as an ad. Period. An ad is interpreted as an ad.

 

[04:07]So, long story short, when you get attention of gym owners who might go to your website, maybe you pixel that specific press release using a tool like Snipply, snip. That enables you to put a pixel over it and also put a little optin box or whatever you want to custom code in to bottom corner, a popup on the page. You can do a bunch of cool stuff with Snippy.

 

[04:31]Long story short, if you get a press release, don't talk about yourself. Get mentioned about what you did and expand the category. Use that run an engagement campaign towards the people that you want to sell, like gym owners in that example.

 

[04:46]After you get the attention from those people who are really looking to better themselves and better their business, generating more leads, generating more sales, if they so happen to click and fill out your form online to talk to you, they'll likely be much more qualified.

 

[05:01]But the whole point of why you're doing this is you're qualifying the attention that comes to you. That way when you advertise, keep in mind, remember the quote, PR is for expanding the category. Advertising is for retaining the attention that was got.

 

[05:17]So from that point forward, when you're running your advertising efforts, you're targeting towards a smaller audience of people who are more qualified, who understand the potential of the result, and who have affinity with the industry that was spoke about.

 

[05:31]You're going to have a higher probability of closing people. It's going to cost you less. And when you run lead generation in that case, it's going to be once again for more qualified leads comparatively to the ice cold leads that you'll be generating if you ever choose to spend money for generating leads in your business.

 

[05:49]I prefer the perfect cold video approach. Just literally having a long list of the top people that I want to work with and taking the sniper approach, making a perfect code video, sending it off to those people and building my clients that way.

 

[06:03]If I were to advertise to generate clients with my agency, I would choose to get a PR piece written about us specifically talking about a large result with a specific service that we want to sell about the industry that we are selling to where we are simply mentioned.

 

[06:21]I want I would want to use that then to have an engagement campaign on Facebook where I would get the attention of the people that I want to sell onto that pixelled on the homepage visiting it interacting with it on social because all that's retargetable.

 

[06:37]And then from there, I'd advertise to those people lead forms, and that's how I would be generating leads if I would take that approach. Hope this helps you in your business.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

9x - Aumento de leads mencionado no exemplo de chatbots

20x - Aumento de receita mencionado no exemplo de chatbots

95% - Percentual do press release focado no processo/categoria

5% - Percentual do press release focado em você/sua agência

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Framework PR vs Advertising: PR expande categoria, Advertising retém atenção

Estratégia de Press Release: Focar 95% no processo/indústria, 5% menção da agência

Headlines focadas em resultado da categoria, não da agência específica

Usar Snipply para pixelar press releases e adicionar opt-in boxes

Engagement Campaign no Facebook direcionado ao público do press release

Retargeting de visitantes pixelados com lead forms

Perfect Cold Video Approach como método preferencial (sniper approach)

Sequência: PR → Engagement Campaign → Pixel → Retargeting → Lead Forms

 

ESTRUTURA:

Modelo de Headline: "Fitness Gyms Are Gaining 9x Leads Using Chatbots" (foco na categoria)

Estrutura do Press Release: 95% processo + categoria / 5% menção com hyperlink

Funil de Qualificação: PR (awareness) → Engagement (interesse) → Retargeting (conversão)

Posicionamento de Expert através de PR vs Ads diretos

Framework de Interpretação Mental: Press Release ≠ Ad (menor resistência)

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

9x aumento em leads (exemplo chatbot para gym)

20x aumento em receita (exemplo chatbot para gym)

Leads mais qualificados = menor custo de aquisição

Leads frios (cold) vs Leads aquecidos (retargeting) - diferença em qualificação

ROI melhorado através de audience qualificado

 

DURAÇÃO: ~6:50 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional direto para câmera, sem slides visuais detectados, com música de introdução
